topic_title: If / else — writing decision logic in code

length_bias.detail: No question has correct answer as longest by more than 20% of mean. Length bias absent.

distractor_plausibility.detail: All distractors capture plausible beginner misconceptions: Q1 (= vs == confusion), Q2 (brace/keyword syntax from other languages), Q3 (wrong assumption about top-to-bottom skipping), Q4 (both-conditions logic error), Q5 (implicit type conversion assumption), Q6 (both-branch-runs misconception). No absurd distractors.

objective_coverage.detail: All 5 learning objectives covered. LO2 tested by 2 questions (indentation rule + predict-output). No question tests out-of-scope material.

beginner_fairness.detail: All stems in plain language. No unexplained jargon. Every question tests only 11.5 scope. Distractors are beginner misconceptions, not advanced-topic trivia.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). 6-MCQ quiz is clean: positional bias max_share=0.333, no length bias, all distractors plausible, all 5 LOs covered. Bias defense applied — positions [0,3,2,1,3,0].
